skill.name: p-image
skill.description: Generate images with Pruna P-Image models via inference.sh CLI. Models: P-Image, P-Image-LoRA, P-Image-Edit, P-Image-Edit-LoRA. Capabilities: text-to-image, image editing, LoRA styles, multi-image compositing, fast inference.
skill.summary: Fast AI image generation using Pruna's optimized models via inference.sh CLI for text-to-image, editing, and stylized outputs.
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected patterns in SKILL.md documentation file, but all findings are false positives. The file contains markdown documentation with code examples showing CLI usage for the inference.sh platform. Backtick characters are markdown formatting for code blocks, not actual shell execution. Hardcoded URLs are documentation links to inference.sh website. No executable code or malicious patterns exist in this skill.

evidence.context: Markdown code block with example infsh login command
Example CLI command for text-to-image generation
Example CLI command for LoRA preset usage
Example CLI command for image editing
Image asset URL for skill preview

content.user_title: Generate AI Images with Pruna P-Image Models
content.value_statement: Create high-quality images quickly using Pruna's optimized AI models. This skill provides fast, cost-effective image generation and editing through the inference.sh CLI platform.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using P-Image and P-Image-LoRA models
Edit existing images with text-based instructions via P-Image-Edit
Apply preset LoRA styles like photos-realism, pixel-art, cinematic-movie-style
Output images in multiple aspect ratios including 1:1, 16:9, 9:16, 4:3
Access optimized FLUX, Qwen, and Z-Image models for faster inference
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and account authentication
Network connection required to access inference.sh API endpoints
Image generation incurs usage costs based on model and resolution selected
LoRA presets are limited to built-in options provided by Pruna

use_cases.title: Marketing Content Creation
use_cases.description: Generate product images, social media visuals, and advertising assets quickly using text prompts or edit existing images with new backgrounds.

use_cases.title: Rapid Prototyping for Design
use_cases.description: Create visual mockups and concept art during early design phases. Apply different LoRA styles to explore visual directions efficiently.

use_cases.title: Batch Image Production
use_cases.description: Generate multiple image variations or composite images at scale using optimized models like WAN Image Small for cost-effective bulk operations.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text-to-Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image with the prompt: a futuristic cityscape at sunset with flying vehicles, cyberpunk style, highly detailed

prompt_templates.title: Styled Portrait with LoRA
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a portrait using P-Image-LoRA with the prompt: professional headshot of a developer in modern office, apply photos-realism preset
prompt_templates.scenario: Generating realistic portraits with specific style

prompt_templates.title: Image Editing Task
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit this image: change the background to a beach sunset while keeping the subject unchanged, maintain original lighting on the person
prompt_templates.scenario: Modifying existing images with text instructions

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Image Composite
prompt_templates.prompt: Combine these three product images into a single collage layout with consistent lighting and professional presentation for e-commerce listing

output_examples.input: Generate a product photo of sneakers with studio lighting, 1:1 aspect ratio
output_examples.output: Returns a high-quality square image showing sneakers with professional studio lighting, neutral background, suitable for e-commerce

output_examples.input: Edit this image to add a mountain background, keep the person in foreground
output_examples.output: Returns edited image with original subject preserved and new mountain landscape background seamlessly integrated

best_practices: Use specific, descriptive prompts with style keywords for better results
Start with P-Image for general generation, then try LoRA variants for specific styles
Test with lower-cost models like FLUX Klein 4B before using premium models for batch work
anti_patterns: Do not use vague prompts like 'make it pretty' without specific visual details
Avoid sending sensitive or private images through the editing API
Do not assume all aspect ratios work equally well for all subjects

faq.question: Do I need to install the inference.sh CLI to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you must have the inference.sh CLI installed and authenticated. Run infsh login after installation to connect your account.

faq.question: What is the difference between P-Image and P-Image-LoRA?
faq.answer: P-Image is for general text-to-image generation. P-Image-LoRA includes preset style filters like photos-realism, pixel-art, and cinematic-movie-style for consistent aesthetic outputs.

faq.question: Can I edit multiple images at once?
faq.answer: Yes, P-Image-Edit supports multi-image compositing. Pass an array of image URLs in the images parameter for collage or combination tasks.

faq.question: How do I check available Pruna models?
faq.answer: Run infsh app list --namespace pruna to see all available Pruna apps and their current status.

faq.question: What aspect ratios are supported?
faq.answer: Supported ratios include 1:1, 16:9, 9:16, 4:3, 3:4, 3:2, 2:3, or custom dimensions by specifying width and height parameters.

faq.question: Is there a free tier for image generation?
faq.answer: Check inference.sh pricing for current plans. Some models like FLUX Klein 4B are positioned as low-cost options at approximately $0.0001 per image.
